3	  id: FEAT-015
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-001

8	---
9	# Feature: DDx Installation Architecture
10	
11	**ID:** FEAT-015
12	**Status:** In Progress
13	**Priority:** P0
14	**Owner:** DDx Team
15	
16	> **Update 2026-04-17:** The skill roster referenced throughout this
17	> document (ddx-bead, ddx-agent, ddx-install, ddx-status, ddx-review,
18	> ddx-run, ddx-doctor) reflects the pre-consolidation layout. Per
19	> FEAT-011, DDx now ships a **single portable `ddx` skill** with
20	> `SKILL.md` + `reference/*.md` (progressive disclosure, agentskills.io
21	> standard). The installation flow described below is still accurate
22	> in structure — binary separate from library, `ddx init` copies skills
23	> as real files, `ddx install <plugin>` adds plugin-scoped content —
24	> but any reference to the 7-skill roster should be read as historical
25	> context. Sections that specifically describe the bootstrap
26	> allowlist are now ["ddx"] instead of ["ddx-doctor", "ddx-run"], and
27	> the stale-skill cleanup removes all old ddx-prefixed dirs (ddx-bead,
28	> ddx-run, ddx-agent, ddx-review, ddx-status, ddx-doctor, ddx-install,
29	> ddx-release) on init and update. See FEAT-011 for the current skill
30	> architecture.
31	
32	## Overview
33	
34	Redesign the DDx installation architecture with a clean separation of concerns:
35	- **install.sh** — binary only (minimal attack surface, fast)
36	- **ddx install --global** — extract embedded skills to `~/.ddx/`, symlink to `~/.agents/` and `~/.claude/`
37	- **ddx init** — project-local skills copied (not symlinked) into `.ddx/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`
38	- **ddx install \<plugin\>** — project-scoped: plugin resources to `.ddx/plugins/`, skills to `.agents/` and `.claude/` via relative symlinks
39	
40	## Problem Statement
41	
42	Current behavior:
43	- `install.sh` does too much (creates `~/.ddx/`, sets up symlinks)
44	- `ddx install helix` clones to user home (`~/.ddx/plugins/`), not project-scoped
45	- Symlinks aren't tracked by git, so project-local `.agents/skills → .ddx/skills` breaks on clone
46	- No separation between global installation and project-scoped plugin management
47	
48	Desired behavior:
49	- `install.sh` does one thing: get the binary into PATH
50	- `ddx install --global` owns global skill setup (home directory)
51	- `ddx init` copies bootstrap skills as real files (git-trackable)
52	- `ddx install <plugin>` is project-scoped, uses relative symlinks for `.agents/` and `.claude/`
53	
54	## Requirements
55	
56	### Functional
57	
58	#### install.sh (curl script)
59	
60	1. **Binary-Only Installation**
61	   - Downloads `ddx` binary to `~/.local/bin/ddx`
62	   - Sets up PATH in shell rc file
63	   - Sets up shell completions
64	   - Does NOT create `~/.ddx/`, `~/.agents/`, or `~/.claude/`
65	
66	#### Global Installation (`ddx install --global`)
67	
68	2. **DDx Skills Extraction**
69	   - Extracts embedded skills (ddx-bead, ddx-agent, ddx-install, ddx-status, ddx-review, ddx-run) to `~/.ddx/skills/`
70	   - Creates `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*` symlinks → `~/.ddx/skills/ddx-*`
71	   - Creates `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` symlinks → `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`
72	
73	#### Repository Initialization (`ddx init`)
74	
75	3. **Project Structure Creation**
76	   - Creates `.ddx/` directory with config.yaml and library structure
77	   - Copies bootstrap skills (ddx-doctor, ddx-run) as **real files** to `.ddx/skills/`
78	   - Copies bootstrap skills to `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` as **real files**
79	   - All files are git-trackable (no symlinks for project-local skills)
80	
81	3a. **Bootstrap Skill Cleanup (Stale ddx-* Removal)**
82	   - Before copying bootstrap skills, scans each target directory (`.ddx/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`) for existing `ddx-*` subdirectories
83	   - Any `ddx-*` directory containing a `SKILL.md` that is **not** in the current bootstrap allowlist (`ddx-doctor`, `ddx-run`) is removed
84	   - Purpose: removes skills from older DDx versions that are no longer part of the bootstrap set
85	   - Only removes `ddx-*` prefixed directories; plugin skills (e.g., `helix-*`) are never touched
86	   - Silent: no user-visible output on cleanup; errors are ignored (non-fatal)
87	   - Runs on every `ddx init` invocation, including `ddx init --force`
88	
89	4. **Pre-flight Check**
90	   - Verify `ddx` binary exists in PATH
91	   - If missing: warn user, suggest running install.sh
92	
93	#### Plugin Installation (`ddx install <plugin>`)
94	
95	5. **Project-Scoped Plugin Install**
96	   - Default: downloads released tarball from plugin's GitHub releases
97	   - Extracts to `$PROJECT/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
98	   - Creates relative symlinks from `.agents/skills/<skill>` → `.ddx/plugins/<name>/.agents/skills/<skill>`
99	   - Creates relative symlinks from `.claude/skills/<skill>` → `.agents/skills/<skill>`
100	   - Relative symlinks work across clone/checkout (no absolute paths)
101	   - Fallback: `ddx install <plugin> --from-source` clones repo (for developers working on the plugin)
102	
103	5a. **Plugin Skill Stale Link Pruning**
104	   - Before creating new skill symlinks, scans the target skills directory for existing entries not in the plugin's current skill list
105	   - Removes a symlink only if **both** conditions hold: (a) it is a symlink (not a real file), and (b) its resolved target is within the plugin's installed root (`.ddx/plugins/<name>/`)
106	   - Purpose: removes symlinks for skills that were removed in a plugin update
107	   - Real files (e.g., bootstrap skills copied by `ddx init`) are never removed by this step
108	   - Symlinks from other plugins (pointing outside this plugin's root) are never removed
109	   - Runs during every `ddx install <plugin>` invocation
110	
111	5b. **Stale Install File Removal**
112	   - The plugin registry tracks the set of files written by each install
113	   - On re-install (update), files from the previous install that are absent from the new install's file list are removed
114	   - Applies to any file tracked in the plugin registry entry (e.g., extracted tarball contents)
115	   - Runs during `ddx install <plugin>` when a prior install record exists
116	
117	6. **Plugin Manifest**
118	   - Records installed plugins in `.ddx/plugins.yaml` or similar
119	   - Tracks name, version, install source (release vs source), install date
120	   - Enables `ddx installed` to show project-scoped plugins
121	   - Enables `ddx outdated` to check for newer released versions
122	
123	#### Version Tracking & Staleness Detection
124	
125	9. **Project Version Stamp (`.ddx/versions.yaml`)**
126	   - System-managed file, separate from user config. Users never edit.
127	   - Written by `ddx init`, committed to git alongside config.yaml
128	   - Contains single field: `ddx_version` — the binary version that initialized/last updated the project
129	   - Schema:
130	     ```yaml
131	     ddx_version: "0.3.0"
132	     ```
133	
134	10. **Version Gate (pre-run, every command)**
135	    - If `.ddx/versions.yaml` does not exist → skip (not a DDx project, or pre-versioning)
136	    - If binary version is `"dev"` → skip (development builds bypass gate)
137	    - If binary version < project's `ddx_version` → **hard error, block execution:**
138	      ```
139	      Error: This project requires DDx v0.4.0 or newer (you have v0.3.0).
140	      Run 'ddx upgrade' to update your DDx binary.
141	      ```
142	    - Exempt commands: `upgrade`, `version`, `doctor`, `init` (must work even when binary is too old)
143	    - Runs in `PersistentPreRunE`, after config init, before update check
144	    - Pure string compare — no network, no disk beyond the config read
145	
146	11. **Staleness Hints (post-run, every command)**
147	    - If binary version > project's `ddx_version` → soft hint:
148	      ```
149	      💡 Project skills from DDx v0.3.0 (you have v0.4.0). Run 'ddx init --force' to update.
150	      ```
151	    - Plugin staleness: compare `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries vs `BuiltinRegistry()` → soft hint:
152	      ```
153	      💡 helix 1.0.0 installed, 2.0.0 available. Run 'ddx install helix' to update.
154	      ```
155	    - Runs in `PersistentPostRunE`, after existing update-available banner
156	    - Pure local comparisons — no network
157	
158	12. **Force Refresh (`ddx init --force`)**
159	    - Overwrites `.ddx/versions.yaml` with current binary version
160	    - Overwrites existing skill files (currently `registerProjectSkills` skips existing files — must add `force` parameter)
161	    - Preserves user config in `.ddx/config.yaml`
162	
163	#### Updates
164	
165	7. **Binary Update (`ddx upgrade`)**
166	   - Checks GitHub for new ddx release (async, 24h cache via `~/.cache/ddx/last-update-check.json`)
167	   - Uses GitHub `releases/latest`, so prereleases do not trigger normal update detection unless intentionally promoted as the latest release
168	   - Version comparison must treat any hyphenated prerelease suffix as older than the matching stable release, with channel ordering `alpha < beta < rc < stable` and numeric ordering within the same channel (`rc2 < rc10`)
169	   - Downloads and replaces binary
170	   - After upgrade, next command in project shows staleness hint (correct: new binary > old `ddx_version`)
171	   - Dogfood installs of prereleases remain possible via explicit version selection, e.g. `DDX_VERSION=v0.3.0-rc1 curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/main/install.sh | bash`
172	
173	8. **Plugin Update (`ddx update <plugin>`)**
174	   - Checks plugin's GitHub releases for newer version
175	   - Downloads new release tarball to `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
176	   - Re-establishes relative symlinks
177	   - `ddx update <plugin> --from-source` re-clones from repo HEAD
178	
179	### Non-Functional
180	
181	- **No Repo Bloat:** plugins live in `.ddx/plugins/` (gitignored or committed per user preference)
182	- **Git-Trackable Skills:** `ddx init` copies real files, not symlinks
183	- **Git-Trackable Versions:** `.ddx/versions.yaml` committed to git — teammates get version gate on clone
184	- **Git-Trackable Execution Evidence:** `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` is
185	  the tracked execute-bead attempt bundle defined in FEAT-006 §"Execute-Bead
186	  Evidence Bundle". `ddx init` and any DDx-managed `.gitignore` template
187	  must leave `.ddx/executions/` trackable so bundles committed by
188	  `execute-bead` survive clones. Only the ignored runtime scratch paths
189	  listed in FEAT-006 (`.ddx/exec-runs.d/`, `.ddx/agent-logs/`,
190	  `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*/`) may be excluded from tracking.
191	- **Relative Symlinks for Plugins:** work across machines, no absolute paths
192	- **No Windows Targets:** relative symlinks are acceptable
193	- **Offline-First:** bootstrap skills work without network; version gate is local-only
194	- **Idempotent:** multiple runs of same command produce same result
195	- **Separation of Concerns:** `.ddx/config.yaml` for user preferences, `.ddx/versions.yaml` for system-managed state, `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` for global plugin state
196	
197	## Architecture
198	
199	### Directory Structure
200	
201	```
202	# Global (via ddx install --global)
203	~/.ddx/
204	├── skills/
205	│   ├── ddx-bead/
206	│   ├── ddx-agent/
207	│   ├── ddx-install/
208	│   ├── ddx-status/
209	│   ├── ddx-review/
210	│   └── ddx-run/
211	└── config.yaml
212	
213	~/.agents/skills/
214	├── ddx-bead/ → ~/.ddx/skills/ddx-bead/
215	├── ddx-agent/ → ~/.ddx/skills/ddx-agent/
216	└── ...
217	
218	~/.claude/skills/
219	├── ddx-bead/ → ~/.agents/skills/ddx-bead/
220	└── ...
221	
222	# Project (via ddx init + ddx install helix)
223	project/
224	├── .ddx/
225	│   ├── config.yaml       (user preferences)
226	│   ├── versions.yaml     (system-managed: ddx_version)
227	│   ├── library/
228	│   ├── skills/
229	│   │   ├── ddx-doctor/   (real files, git-tracked)
230	│   │   └── ddx-run/      (real files, git-tracked)
231	│   ├── executions/       (tracked execute-bead attempt bundles; see FEAT-006)
232	│   │   └── <attempt-id>/ (prompt.md, manifest.json, result.json, ...)
233	│   └── plugins/
234	│       └── helix/        (cloned plugin)
235	│           └── .agents/skills/
236	│               ├── helix-align/
237	│               ├── helix-build/
238	│               └── ...
239	├── .agents/skills/
240	│   ├── ddx-doctor/       (real files, copied by ddx init)
241	│   ├── ddx-run/          (real files, copied by ddx init)
242	│   ├── helix-align/ → ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align
243	│   ├── helix-build/ → ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-build
244	│   └── ...
245	├── .claude/skills/
246	│   ├── ddx-doctor/ → ../.agents/skills/ddx-doctor
247	│   ├── helix-align/ → ../.agents/skills/helix-align
248	│   └── ...
249	└── ...
250	```
251	
252	### Command Matrix
253	
254	| Command | Scope | What It Does |
255	|---------|-------|--------------|
256	| `curl install.sh \| bash` | Global | Binary to `~/.local/bin/ddx` + PATH |
257	| `ddx install --global` | Global | Extract skills to `~/.ddx/`, symlink `~/.agents/`, `~/.claude/` |
258	| `ddx init` | Project | `.ddx/` structure + copy bootstrap skills to `.agents/`, `.claude/` |
259	| `ddx install helix` | Project | Clone to `.ddx/plugins/helix/`, relative symlinks in `.agents/`, `.claude/` |
260	| `ddx upgrade` | Global | Update binary |
261	| `ddx update <plugin>` | Project | Re-clone plugin, re-establish symlinks |
262	
263	### Key Design Decisions
264	
265	1. **Copy over symlink for ddx init**: Git doesn't track symlinks well. Bootstrap skills must survive `git clone` on a fresh machine.
266	
267	2. **Relative symlinks for plugins**: Plugin skills are installed via relative symlinks (e.g., `../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align`). This works across machines without absolute paths. Acceptable since we're not targeting Windows.
268	
269	3. **Project-scoped plugins**: Plugins install to the project, not globally. This lets different projects use different plugin versions and makes the project self-contained.
270	
271	4. **Minimal install.sh**: The curl script does one thing (install binary). Everything else is handled by `ddx` commands that have proper error handling, embedded assets, and testability.
272	
273	## Out of Scope
274	
275	- Windows-specific installation (future)
276	- System package manager integration (apt, brew, etc.) (future)
277	- Plugin publishing to registry (future)
278	- Global plugin installation (future — currently project-scoped only)
279	
280	## Acceptance Criteria
281	
282	### AC-001: Clean Machine Installation
283	```bash
284	# In Docker container with nothing installed
285	curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/main/install.sh | bash
286	which ddx            # → ~/.local/bin/ddx
287	ddx version          # → shows version
288	ls ~/.ddx/ 2>&1      # → no such directory (install.sh doesn't create it)
289	```
290	
291	### AC-002: Global Skill Installation
292	Post-consolidation (FEAT-011, 2026-04-17): the embedded tree is the
293	single portable `ddx` skill. Adjust the example from the pre-consolidation
294	`ddx-bead`/`ddx-agent`/etc. listing to match the current layout:
295	
296	```bash
297	# After AC-001
298	ddx install --global
299	ls ~/.ddx/skills/ddx/           # → skill files exist (SKILL.md, reference/*.md)
300	readlink ~/.agents/skills/ddx   # → ../../.ddx/skills/ddx
301	readlink ~/.claude/skills/ddx   # → ../../.agents/skills/ddx
302	```
303	
304	Implementation: `cli/cmd/install_global.go`. Covered by
305	`TestInstallGlobalExtractsSkillsAndChainsSymlinks` and the adjacent
306	idempotency / safety tests.
307	
308	### AC-003: Repository Initialization
309	```bash
310	# In empty project directory
311	ddx init
312	ls .ddx/skills/ddx-doctor/   # → real files (not symlinks)
313	ls .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/ # → real files (copied, not symlinked)
314	ls .claude/skills/ddx-doctor/ # → real files or relative symlink to .agents
315	git add .agents/ .claude/     # → works (real files tracked by git)
316	```
317	
318	### AC-004: Plugin Installation (Project-Scoped)
319	```bash
320	# In initialized project
321	ddx install helix
322	ls .ddx/plugins/helix/                        # → plugin cloned
323	readlink .agents/skills/helix-align           # → ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align
324	readlink .claude/skills/helix-align           # → ../.agents/skills/helix-align
325	```
326	
327	### AC-005: Missing DDx Detection
328	```bash
329	# Clone a project with .ddx/skills/ddx-doctor/ but no ddx binary
330	# ddx-doctor skill detects missing binary and prompts install
331	```
332	
333	### AC-006: Version Tracking
334	```bash
335	# ddx init writes versions.yaml
336	ddx init
337	cat .ddx/versions.yaml  # → ddx_version: "0.3.0" (current binary version)
338	git log --oneline -1     # → commit includes .ddx/versions.yaml
339	```
340	
341	### AC-007: Version Gate (binary too old)
342	```bash
343	# Simulate: edit versions.yaml to require newer version
344	echo 'ddx_version: "99.0.0"' > .ddx/versions.yaml
345	ddx bead list            # → Error: This project requires DDx v99.0.0 or newer...
346	ddx version              # → works (exempt command)
347	ddx upgrade              # → works (exempt command)
348	```
349	
350	### AC-008: Staleness Hint (binary newer)
351	```bash
352	# Simulate: edit versions.yaml to older version
353	echo 'ddx_version: "0.0.1"' > .ddx/versions.yaml
354	ddx bead list            # → normal output + hint: "💡 Project skills from DDx v0.0.1..."
355	```
356	
357	### AC-009: Force Refresh
358	```bash
359	# After staleness hint
360	ddx init --force
361	cat .ddx/versions.yaml   # → ddx_version updated to current
362	cat .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/SKILL.md  # → overwritten with latest
363	```
364	
365	### AC-010: Dev Build Bypass
366	```bash
367	# Dev build (version="dev") should not trigger gate
368	# Even if versions.yaml says v99.0.0
369	ddx bead list            # → works normally, no error
370	```
371	
372	### AC-011: Docker Test Coverage
373	All above scenarios run in Docker containers:
374	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.clean` — minimal image for AC-001
375	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.with-ddx` — pre-installed for AC-002, AC-003, AC-004
376	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.no-binary` — ddx removed for AC-005
377	
378	### AC-012: Bootstrap Skill Cleanup on `ddx init`
379	```bash
380	# Simulate a stale bootstrap skill from an older DDx version
381	mkdir -p .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill
382	echo "---" > .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill/SKILL.md
383	ddx init
384	ls .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill 2>&1  # → no such file or directory (removed)
385	ls .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/         # → present (in bootstrap allowlist)
386	ls .agents/skills/ddx-run/            # → present (in bootstrap allowlist)
387	# Plugin skills are untouched
388	ls .agents/skills/helix-align 2>&1    # → unchanged (not a ddx-* prefix)
389	```
390	
391	### AC-013: Plugin Skill Stale Link Pruning on `ddx install`
392	```bash
393	# Install plugin, then re-install after a skill is removed upstream
394	ddx install helix                         # installs helix-align, helix-build
395	# Simulate helix-build removed in new version (re-install with fewer skills)
396	ddx install helix
397	ls .agents/skills/helix-build 2>&1        # → no such file or directory (stale link removed)
398	ls .agents/skills/helix-align             # → symlink present (still in plugin)
399	# Bootstrap skills are not removed
400	ls .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/             # → unchanged (real file, not symlink)
401	```
402	
403	### AC-014: Stale Install File Removal on Plugin Update
404	```bash
405	# Install plugin, verify old files removed on update
406	ddx install helix@1.0.0
407	# Files from 1.0.0 that don't exist in 1.1.0 are removed when updating
408	ddx install helix@1.1.0
409	# Files only in 1.0.0 are gone; files in 1.1.0 are present
410	```
411

129:  (symlinks break after `git clone` on a fresh machine).

110	
111	> "Before responding to any DDx-related request, read the matching
112	> reference file below. The router is not optional — your answer must
113	> be grounded in the reference file's guidance, not this overview
114	> alone."
115	
116	### Subagents: ship none
117	
118	Subagent orchestration is harness-specific (Claude Code has
119	`.claude/agents/` + `context: fork`; Codex has its own subagent
120	surface; others differ). The `ddx` skill describes *actions*
121	("run `ddx agent run --quorum=<policy>`") and lets each harness
122	decide how to run them. Quorum review is a CLI invocation, not a
123	skill-frontmatter directive.
124	
125	### Installation
126	
127	- `ddx init` copies `skills/ddx/` into `.claude/skills/ddx/`,
128	  `.agents/skills/ddx/`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` as real files
129	  (symlinks break after `git clone` on a fresh machine).
130	- On init and on `ddx update`, stale ddx-prefixed skill directories
131	  from prior DDx versions are removed:
132	  `ddx-bead`, `ddx-run`, `ddx-agent`, `ddx-review`, `ddx-status`,
133	  `ddx-doctor`, `ddx-install`, `ddx-release`. Third-party skills are
134	  untouched.
135	- Skills embed into the binary via `//go:embed all:ddx` against a
136	  copy under `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`. Because `go:embed` cannot
137	  traverse upward, a `make copy-skills` target rsyncs
138	  `skills/ddx/` → `cli/internal/skills/ddx/` before every build.
139	
140	### AGENTS.md: merge, not clobber
141	
142	Codex treats `AGENTS.md` as primary guidance before work, and users
143	may have added content. `ddx init` uses marker-delimited injection:
144	
145	```markdown
146	<!-- DDX-AGENTS:START -->
147	This project uses DDx. Use the `ddx` skill for beads, work, review,
148	agents, and status.
149	
150	(tracker/merge policy follows)
151	<!-- DDX-AGENTS:END -->
152	```
153	
154	Content outside the markers is preserved. The block says
155	"the `ddx` skill", not "`/ddx`" (which is Claude-specific slash-
156	command phrasing). Re-running `ddx init` updates the block in place.
157	
158	### Evaluation-driven validation
159

25:- HELIX publishes skills independently (`~/.agents/skills/`) with no DDx integration
95:    target: ~/.agents/skills/   # Install destination
112:4. Copy skills to `~/.agents/skills/helix-*`
114:6. Record installation in `~/.ddx/installed.yaml`
128:- **Installation record:** `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` (what's installed, versions, timestamps, source registry)

1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: FEAT-009
4	  depends_on:
5	    - helix.prd
6	---
7	# Feature: Online Library & Plugin Registry
8	
9	**ID:** FEAT-009
10	**Status:** Complete
11	**Priority:** P0
12	**Owner:** DDx Team
13	
14	## Overview
15	
16	DDx needs to discover, install, and manage resources from an online library. The library is a git repository (`ddx-library`) containing personas, prompts, templates, patterns, MCP configurations, and **package descriptors** for workflow tools like HELIX. DDx fetches resources on demand — no git subtree, no full repo clone.
17	
18	This replaces the current git-subtree-based sync model with a lighter, more practical approach: DDx downloads what you ask for, caches it locally, and keeps track of what's installed.
19	
20	## Problem Statement
21	
22	**Current situation:**
23	- `ddx-library` exists as a git repo but DDx's sync mechanism (`ddx update`) is a stub
24	- Git subtree is complex, fragile, and overkill for distributing personas and templates
25	- HELIX publishes skills independently (`~/.agents/skills/`) with no DDx integration
26	- Dun expects plugins at `~/.cache/ddx/library/plugins/` but nothing populates this path
27	- There's no way to discover what's available or install a specific resource
28	
29	**Desired outcome:** `ddx install helix` fetches and installs HELIX skills. `ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer` fetches one persona. `ddx search testing` finds testing-related resources. Simple, practical, no git subtree.
30	
31	## Architecture
32	
33	### Registries
34	
35	DDx supports multiple registries. Each registry is a git repository containing a `registry.yaml` index and installable resources. Registries are checked in order — first match wins.
36	
37	```yaml
38	# .ddx/config.yaml
39	registries:
40	  - url: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library     # default, always present
41	    branch: main
42	  - url: https://github.com/mycompany/ddx-private  # company-private
43	    branch: main
44	```
45	
46	The default registry (`https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library`) is always included even if not explicitly listed. Additional registries are additive — they extend the default, not replace it.
47	
48	### Registry Repository Structure
49	
50	Each registry repo has:
51	
52	```
53	ddx-library/
54	├── registry.yaml              # Index of all available packages
55	├── personas/
56	│   ├── strict-code-reviewer.md
57	│   ├── pragmatic-implementer.md
58	│   └── ...
59	├── prompts/
60	│   └── ...
61	├── templates/
62	│   └── ...
63	├── patterns/
64	│   └── ...
65	├── artifacts/                 # Artifact type resources
66	│   ├── adr/
67	│   │   ├── template.md
68	│   │   ├── create.md
69	│   │   ├── evolve.md
70	│   │   └── check.md
71	│   └── ...
72	├── mcp-servers/
73	│   └── registry.yml
74	├── workflows/
75	│   └── helix/
76	│       └── package.yaml       # HELIX package descriptor
77	└── plugins/
78	    └── helix/
79	        └── plugin.yaml        # Dun plugin for HELIX checks
80	```
81	
82	### Package Descriptor
83	
84	Workflow tools and plugins publish a `package.yaml` in the library:
85	
86	```yaml
87	name: helix
88	version: 1.0.0
89	description: Structured development workflow with AI-assisted collaboration
90	type: workflow                  # workflow | plugin | persona-pack | template-pack
91	source: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/helix
92	install:
93	  skills:
94	    source: .agents/skills/     # Path in source repo
95	    target: ~/.agents/skills/   # Install destination
96	  scripts:
97	    source: scripts/helix
98	    target: ~/.local/bin/helix
99	requires:
100	  - ddx >= 0.2.0
101	```
102	
103	### Install Flow
104	
105	```bash
106	ddx install helix
107	```
108	
109	1. Fetch `registry.yaml` from ddx-library (cached, refreshed on `ddx update`)
110	2. Find the `helix` entry → read `package.yaml`
111	3. Clone/download the source repo (shallow, to temp dir)
112	4. Copy skills to `~/.agents/skills/helix-*`
113	5. Copy scripts to `~/.local/bin/helix`
114	6. Record installation in `~/.ddx/installed.yaml`
115	
116	For simple resources (individual personas, templates):
117	
118	```bash
119	ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer
120	```
121	
122	1. Fetch the file directly from ddx-library (via GitHub raw URL or git archive)
123	2. Copy to `.ddx/library/personas/strict-code-reviewer.md`
124	
125	### Cache and State
126	
127	- **Registry cache:** `~/.cache/ddx/registries/<name>/registry.yaml` (one per registry, refreshed by `ddx update`)
128	- **Installation record:** `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` (what's installed, versions, timestamps, source registry)
129	- **Library cache:** `~/.cache/ddx/library/` (downloaded resources)
130	- **Plugin cache:** `~/.cache/ddx/library/plugins/` (populated for dun discovery)
131	
132	## Requirements
133	
134	### Functional
135	
136	1. **Registry fetch** (`ddx update`) — download latest `registry.yaml` from ddx-library
137	2. **Search** (`ddx search <query>`) — search available resources by name, type, or keyword
138	3. **Install resource** (`ddx install <name>`) — download and install a specific resource or package
139	4. **Install workflow** (`ddx install helix`) — full workflow installation (skills, scripts, plugins)
140	5. **List installed** (`ddx installed`) — show what's installed locally
141	6. **Uninstall** (`ddx uninstall <name>`) — remove an installed resource
142	7. **Populate plugin cache** — on install, copy dun-compatible plugins to `~/.cache/ddx/library/plugins/`
143	8. **Version tracking** — record installed versions, detect available updates
144	9. **Update detection** (`ddx outdated`) — compare installed package versions
145	   against source repo tags (via `git ls-remote --tags`) to determine if
146	   updates are available. Output: package name, installed version, latest
147	   available, update available (yes/no).
148	10. **Package update** (`ddx upgrade <name>`) — re-install a package at the
149	    latest available version. Performs a fresh shallow clone at the latest tag,
150	    copies new files, updates `installed.yaml`. Safe to run repeatedly.
151	11. **Startup update check** — on `ddx` startup (async, non-blocking), check
152	    if installed packages have newer versions available. If so, print a
153	    one-line notice: `Plugin update available: helix 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 (run
154	    'ddx upgrade helix')`. Same pattern as the existing DDx binary update
155	    check.
156	
157	### Non-Functional
158	
159	- **No git subtree** — fetch individual files or shallow clones, not full repo history
160	- **Offline-safe** — work from cache when offline; warn but don't fail
161	- **Idempotent** — running `ddx install helix` twice is safe
162	- **Fast** — individual resource install <5s on broadband
163	
164	## CLI Commands
165	
166	```bash
167	ddx update                          # Refresh registry from upstream
168	ddx search <query>                  # Search available resources
169	ddx install <name>                  # Install a resource or package
170	ddx install helix                   # Install HELIX workflow
171	ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer  # Install one persona
172	ddx installed                       # List what's installed
173	ddx outdated                        # Check for available updates
174	ddx upgrade <name>                  # Update a package to latest version
175	ddx uninstall <name>                # Remove an installed resource
176	```
177	
178	## User Stories
179	
180	### US-090: Developer Discovers Available Workflows
181	**As a** developer evaluating DDx
182	**I want** to search for available workflow tools
183	**So that** I can find and install HELIX or other methodologies
184	
185	**Acceptance Criteria:**
186	- Given I run `ddx search workflow`, then I see HELIX and any other registered workflows with descriptions
187	- Given I run `ddx install helix`, then HELIX skills are installed to `~/.agents/skills/` and the CLI to `~/.local/bin/`
188	
189	### US-091: Developer Installs Individual Resources
190	**As a** developer customizing my project
191	**I want** to install specific personas or templates from the library
192	**So that** I get exactly what I need without bulk downloading
193	
194	**Acceptance Criteria:**
195	- Given I run `ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer`, then the persona file is copied to `.ddx/library/personas/`
196	- Given I run `ddx installed`, then I see `persona/strict-code-reviewer` with version and install date
197	
198	## Migration from Git Subtree
199	
200	The current `ddx init` creates a git subtree for `.ddx/library/`. This should be replaced:

input.new_string:
> **Update 2026-04-29 (project-local install, no symlinks):** This feature
> is amended to drop the global skill installation model entirely. DDx no
> longer installs skills under `~/.agents/skills/` or `~/.claude/skills/`,
> and `ddx install --global` is removed. `ddx init` and `ddx install
> <plugin>` only touch the current `<projectRoot>`. The new project-local
> tree (`<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`,
> `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`) is committed to git as real files.
> **Zero symlinks are created by ddx install** — see the new
> "Cross-platform invariant" section below for the Windows-compatibility
> reasoning and how plugin-author symlinks are handled. Existing projects
> with home-directory symlinks or stale `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries
> are handled per the new "Migration" section. Plugin manifests whose
> `Root.Target` begins with `~` are rejected with a non-zero exit.
> Sections, bullets, and acceptance criteria below that describe
> `ddx install --global`, `~/.ddx/skills/`, `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`,
> or `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` should be read as historical context;
> the amendment in this header and the two new sections are
> authoritative.
>
> **Update 2026-04-17:** The skill roster referenced throughout this

input.old_string:
## Overview

Redesign the DDx installation architecture with a clean separation of concerns:
- **install.sh** — binary only (minimal attack surface, fast)
- **ddx install --global** — extract embedded skills to `~/.ddx/`, symlink to `~/.agents/` and `~/.claude/`
- **ddx init** — project-local skills copied (not symlinked) into `.ddx/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`
- **ddx install \<plugin\>** — project-scoped: plugin resources to `.ddx/plugins/`, skills to `.agents/` and `.claude/` via relative symlinks
input.new_string:
## Overview

Redesign the DDx installation architecture with a clean separation of concerns:
- **install.sh** — binary only (minimal attack surface, fast)
- **ddx init** — project-local: writes `<projectRoot>/.ddx/`,
  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`
  as real files (copied, never symlinked). Touches no path outside the
  project root.
- **ddx install \<plugin\>** — project-local: plugin tarball extracted
  into `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, then plugin skills copied
  as real files into `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and
  `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. Touches no path outside the project
  root. Creates zero symlinks (see "Cross-platform invariant").
- All project-local install outputs (`<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`,
  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`)
  are intended to be committed to git so teammates and CI get the
  same skills on clone without re-running `ddx install`.

## Cross-platform invariant

**`ddx install` (and `ddx init`) creates zero symlinks.** Every file
written is a real file copied from the binary's embedded tree or from
an extracted plugin tarball.

**Reasoning — Windows compatibility.** Symlinks on Windows require
either Developer Mode, an elevated process, or specific filesystem
features, and behave inconsistently across NTFS, ReFS, and shared
filesystems. A single project-local layout that works identically on
Linux, macOS, and Windows is worth the duplication cost of copied
files.

**Plugin-source symlinks are not materialized.** If a plugin tarball
contains symlinks (e.g. an author symlinks `.claude/skills/foo` →
`../.agents/skills/foo` inside their source tree), DDx does **not**
materialize those symlinks on extract. Plugin authors are responsible
for shipping each skill as a real directory in every target the
plugin declares. A plugin that relies on tarball symlinks resolving
on the install side is a malformed plugin.

**Manifest validation.** Plugin manifests are scanned at install
time. Any `Root.Target` (or equivalent install target) whose path
begins with `~` is rejected and `ddx install` exits with a non-zero
status. Targets must be project-relative paths under
`<projectRoot>/`. This invariant is enforced even for plugins that
worked under the pre-amendment global model.

## Migration

Pre-amendment installs may have left state in three places. This
section describes how DDx heals or ignores each.

- **Home-directory symlinked skills** (`~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`,
  `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` pointing into `~/.ddx/skills/` or a project
  tree). `ddx update --force` (and `ddx init --force` inside an
  existing project) heals the project by rewriting
  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`
  as real files. DDx does **not** delete files outside the project
  root; users may `rm -rf ~/.agents/skills/ddx-*
  ~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` themselves if they want a clean home tree.

- **Stale `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries** from a prior global
  install. These are treated as **no-op uninstalls**: `ddx
  uninstall <name>` succeeds without touching the filesystem when
  the recorded paths are outside the current project root, and the
  entry is removed from `installed.yaml`. No error is raised for
  missing files.

- **`~/.ddx/plugins/`** from a prior global install. DDx leaves this
  directory in place as inert state — nothing reads from it under
  the project-local model. Users may `rm -rf ~/.ddx/plugins/` at
  their convenience; DDx will not recreate it.

> `ddx install`, and the no-`~` manifest invariant.

  `.agents/skills/ddx/`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` as real files
  (symlinks break after `git clone` on a fresh machine, and DDx no
  longer creates symlinks during install — see FEAT-015).

input.new_string:
### Install Flow

> **Amended by FEAT-015 (2026-04-29):** `ddx install <plugin>` is now
> project-local. Skills land in `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and
> `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/` as real files (no symlinks, no home-
> directory writes). The `~/.agents/skills/` / `~/.local/bin/helix`
> targets and `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` flow described below are
> historical context from the pre-amendment global model. See FEAT-015
> for the authoritative install semantics and the no-`~` manifest
> invariant.

```bash
ddx install helix
```

1. Fetch `registry.yaml` from ddx-library (cached, refreshed on `ddx update`)
2. Find the `helix` entry → read `package.yaml`
3. Clone/download the source repo (shallow, to temp dir)
4. Copy skills to `~/.agents/skills/helix-*`
5. Copy scripts to `~/.local/bin/helix`
6. Record installation in `~/.ddx/installed.yaml`

git add docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-009-library-registry.md && git commit -m "docs(FEAT-015): amend install architecture for project-local, no-symlinks model [ddx-2d9026d0]

Drop the global skill installation model from FEAT-015. ddx install/init
now only touch <projectRoot>; zero symlinks are created by ddx install
(Windows compat); plugin-source symlinks are not materialized; manifests
with ~-prefixed Root.Target are rejected. Adds Cross-platform invariant
and Migration sections; updates FEAT-011 and FEAT-009 cross-references
to point at the FEAT-015 amendment.

Parent epic: ddx-3562c48c"
